$comment: Curated compatibility database for known-troublesome Windows apps under Wine, per DESIGN.md §2: 'a compatibility database/launcher (similar in spirit to Lutris' install scripts) curated for your distro specifically, so common known-troublesome apps get auto-applied workarounds on install.' Entries are limited to apps/fixes with well-established, widely-documented behavior (WineHQ AppDB consensus, winetricks' own verb set) -- status 'native-alternative-recommended' apps point at a real native Linux option instead of fabricating a Wine fix that doesn't reliably exist. Loaded by bin/distro-gaming-compat. Each 'winetricks_verbs' entry is a real, current winetricks verb name -- not invented.

apps.name: Microsoft Office 2010/2013/2016 (classic MSI installer, not Click-to-Run/365)
apps.notes: Classic MSI-based Office installs are the long-documented WineHQ AppDB baseline case for this verb set. Click-to-Run (modern 365 installer) and license activation are far less reliable -- don't expect this to cover those.
apps.source: WineHQ AppDB general guidance for the MS Office product line

apps.name: Microsoft Office 365 (Click-to-Run installer)
apps.notes: Click-to-Run's installer and licensing/activation flow are not reliably functional under Wine. Use the classic MSI offline installer if you have access to one (see ms-office-classic), or use Office's web apps (office.com) in a browser, which work natively and fully.
apps.source: Long-standing WineHQ AppDB consensus on Click-to-Run vs classic MSI Office installers

native_alternative.notes: GNOME's native PDF viewer (already pulled in by ubuntu-desktop-minimal/lubuntu-desktop on most strains) handles standard PDFs, forms, and annotations. Reader DC under Wine is a known-poor experience (installer issues, plugin/update-service crashes) with no reliable fix.

apps.name: Adobe Creative Cloud desktop apps (Photoshop CC, Illustrator CC, Premiere/After Effects)
apps.notes: The Creative Cloud installer/licensing service and the apps themselves are not reliably functional under Wine -- this is the same DRM/licensing-service problem flagged in DESIGN.md §2/§4 for Premiere/After Effects. Don't attempt; see modes/creative/README.md for the native alternatives Crucible OS actually ships (FreeCAD, Blender, DaVinci Resolve, Kdenlive).

apps.name: Apple iTunes
apps.notes: Long-standing, never-fully-resolved WineHQ AppDB case -- installer/service crashes, Apple Mobile Device Support failures. No reliable workaround exists; there's also no good native-Linux equivalent for iOS device sync specifically. If only used for music playback, a native player (Rhythmbox/Clementine) plus moving the music library off-device is the realistic path, not a Wine fix.

apps.name: Epic Games Launcher

native_alternative.notes: Heroic Games Launcher is a native, actively-maintained open-source client for Epic (and GOG/Amazon) game libraries -- a far more reliable path than running Epic's own launcher under Wine, which has long-standing UI rendering and login issues.

apps.source: Heroic Games Launcher project (heroicgameslauncher.com); widely known as the standard Linux alternative to Epic's launcher

native_alternative.notes: Heroic Games Launcher also covers GOG library management/installs natively. GOG itself also sells DRM-free installers that work fine standalone without Galaxy at all.

apps.name: Battle.net launcher (Blizzard)
apps.notes: Use Lutris' own maintained Battle.net install script rather than a hand-rolled prefix -- Lutris tracks Blizzard's frequent launcher updates more closely than a static verb list here ever could. This entry exists so the database is honest about the recommended path (defer to Lutris) instead of pretending to have a static fix.
apps.source: Lutris install-script database (lutris.net) maintains the current Battle.net recipe

apps.name: Discord

native_alternative.notes: Discord ships an official native Linux client (also available as a .deb from discord.com or via Flathub). No reason to run the Windows build under Wine.

apps.source: Discord's own official Linux downloads page

apps.name: Spotify

native_alternative.notes: Spotify ships an official native Linux client (Snap, or Flathub). No reason to run the Windows build under Wine.

apps.source: Spotify's own official Linux downloads page

apps.name: Generic .NET Framework desktop app (no native Linux build)
apps.notes: Not a specific app -- a generic starting point for the very common 'this one app needs .NET and a specific font' case DESIGN.md §2 calls out winetricks as the answer for. Try this verb set first for unlisted .NET-based Windows utilities before assuming Wine can't run it at all.
